scenarios.task: We spend $150k/month across paid channels. Blended CAC went from $95 to $133 over two quarters. The agency says we should increase the Meta budget because its platform-reported ROAS is 4.2, our best channel. Advise.
criteria: Refuses to act on blended CAC and demands channel-level CAC decomposition to find which channel is decaying
Distrusts platform-reported (attributed) ROAS specifically and raises incrementality (e.g. would those conversions have happened anyway)
Flags branded search / retargeting as common attribution inflators to check before crediting a channel
Considers marginal CAC — the next dollar into Meta buys worse performance than the average suggests if the audience is saturating
Proposes a concrete test (holdout/geo test) before reallocating a large budget on attribution data

scenarios.task: B2B SaaS, $3M ARR, growing but slowing. Our messaging says we're an 'all-in-one work management platform.' We compete against Asana and Monday with 1% of their brand. The founder wants a rebrand with a bigger 'awareness' campaign. What's your take?
criteria: Diagnoses positioning before proposing spend: an undifferentiated 'all-in-one' claim against giant incumbents is the core problem
Recommends narrowing to a segment/use-case where the product wins decisively rather than out-shouting incumbents on the same claim
Applies a structured positioning process (competitive alternatives, unique capabilities, who cares most) rather than wordsmithing a tagline
Requires any awareness spend to have a measurement plan before committing budget
Questions whether awareness is the binding constraint at $3M ARR versus conversion/retention of existing demand

scenarios.task: Our e-commerce brand has been running 25%-off sitewide promos almost monthly for a year. Revenue holds steady, but the CFO says contribution margin is eroding. Growth team says promos 'work' because each one spikes sales. What do you tell them?
criteria: Identifies that regular promos train customers to wait for discounts, pulling forward full-price demand rather than creating demand
Asks for the analysis that separates incremental revenue from cannibalized full-price revenue in the promo spikes
Connects the discounting pattern to margin math explicitly (what a 25% discount does to contribution at their margin structure)
Names the long-term brand/price-integrity cost of monthly sitewide discounting as strategy
Proposes a controlled path out (targeted/segmented offers, promo holdout tests, cadence reduction) instead of just 'stop discounting'
